# OQS status meeting – 2026-06-16 – summary

*Note: Generated by AI based on the agenda and transcript.*

## Attendees

- Bruce Xu (University of Waterloo)
- Douglas Stebila (University of Waterloo)
- Rodrigo Martín (Indra)

**Date and time:** Tuesday, June 16, 2026, 12:30–13:00 PM US Eastern / 9:30 AM US Pacific / 18:30 Central European

**Location:** Zoom

Information about the OQS project is available at <https://openquantumsafe.org>. The project dashboard is at <https://openquantumsafe.org/dashboard.html>.

## OQS Technical Steering Committee

Rodrigo encouraged all team members to engage with the two open TSC issues: the TSC vice-chair selection ([issue #295](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/295)) and the removal of the security email list ([issue #293](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/293)). With summer approaching, making progress on the vice-chair discussion before the next TSC meeting is a priority. Rodrigo also noted that the broader question of how to handle security reports in general — beyond the specific email list question — was not fully resolved at the June 2 TSC meeting and should be discussed further.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (all):** Comment on the TSC vice-chair selection ([issue #295](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/295)) and the security email list ([issue #293](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/293)) ahead of the next TSC meeting.

## liboqs

### PR [#2407](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2407) — HQC update (0.16 blocker)

Bruce confirmed that the recent changes to the HQC PR are purely a rebase on top of recently merged PRs (notably the NTRU KEM security fix from [PR #2460](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2460)), resolving the resulting merge conflicts. There is no meaningful change to the HQC integration itself. Rodrigo and Douglas both have outstanding re-approvals to give following the rebase; Douglas confirmed he will review it again. This remains the main blocker for the 0.16 release.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Rodrigo, Douglas):** Re-review and re-approve [PR #2407](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2407) to unblock the 0.16 release.

### liboqs 0.16.0 release candidate ([PR #2451](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2451))

Bruce reported that the release candidate PR is nearly ready. Release notes are approximately 99% complete — the HQC section will be added once [PR #2407](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2407) merges. A checklist of remaining outstanding items is tracked in the RC PR itself. Bruce's goal is to have all checkboxes cleared and the PR moved to ready-for-review by the end of this week. He confirmed he does not need external help on the remaining items at this point.

### Intel SDE CI fix ([PR #2463](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2463), merged)

The weekly build failures caused by Intel's download mirror blocking automated access ([issue #2436](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/issues/2436)) have been resolved. The Intel Software Development Emulator binary, together with a copy of its license, has been added to the OQS CI containers repository ([PR #103](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/ci-containers/pull/103) and [PR #104](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/ci-containers/pull/104), both merged). The liboqs CI workflows now pull from the OQS-hosted copy rather than from Intel's servers. Rodrigo noted that a concern was raised (by Michael, via email) about the licensing implications of hosting the binary; Bruce confirmed that including a copy of the Intel Simplified Software License resolves this.

### PR [#2441](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2441) — Algorithm support table (merged)

This PR has been merged. Rodrigo noted that a small number of algorithm entries still lack a confirmed maintainer point of contact — in particular FrodoKEM (which Rodrigo will follow up on with Douglas) and several algorithms originating from the PQClean repository that do not have a clear upstream successor. These will be handled as follow-on issues.

### Trail of Bits collaboration

A kickoff meeting with the Trail of Bits engineer is scheduled for next Monday (June 22) at 10:00 AM US Eastern. Douglas and Bruce discussed priorities beforehand: the focus will be on understanding how to use modern AI-assisted tooling for security review, getting advice on processes for handling and prioritising incoming security reports, and general security assessment — rather than specifically tasking the engineer with fixing individual bugs. Rodrigo confirmed he will join the call as additional support, given the relevance of the security process discussion to his TSC chair responsibilities.

## OQS-OpenSSL 3 provider

### PR [#778](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/pull/778) — Avoid encoder hotpath in P-curve hybrid KEM keygen

Rodrigo reviewed the PR over the past week and has concerns about whether the performance improvement claimed by the author is genuine. His own testing was unable to reproduce the baseline performance problem that motivated the PR. He noted that interactions around this PR have been somewhat unusual. He has posted his findings as a comment on the PR and is waiting for a response. Rodrigo asked Norman and Michael to also take a look and share their assessment. This will be followed up at next week's meeting when more attendees are likely to be present.

### Issues [#783](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/issues/783) and [#784](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/issues/784)

Two new issues were filed: one flagging confusing information about SLH-DSA (and hybrids) in TLS, and one reporting that modifications to the `generate.yml` template are not working as expected. Rodrigo noted he has asked whether the reporters would be willing to submit PRs and is awaiting responses.

## OQS-OpenSSH

[PR #194](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/openssh/pull/194) (uplift to OpenSSH 10.3p1) was merged earlier today. No further items to discuss.

## OQS-BoringSSL, oqs-demos, ci-containers, www, language wrappers

No updates were raised for these subprojects beyond what is noted above regarding the Intel SDE CI fix (ci-containers, www). Douglas confirmed there is nothing further to report on the Python wrapper at this time.

## Next meetings

Next OQS status meeting: Tuesday, June 23, 2026, at 12:30 PM US Eastern.
